benchmark_details.name: H ALLU CODE
benchmark_details.overview: H ALLU CODE is a benchmark for evaluating the performance of code LLMs in recognizing hallucinations in generated code, comprising 5,663 Python code generation tasks, reference solutions, and their corresponding hallucinated counterparts.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To evaluate the performance of code LLMs in recognizing hallucinations and develop effective techniques for their detection and mitigation.

data.source: Generated from the Code Alpaca dataset, which contains instruction-following data for code generation.
data.annotation: Manually analyzed by annotators with programming experience to identify hallucinations.

methodology.calculation: Metrics calculated based on the percentage of correctly identified hallucinations and their types in the generated code.
methodology.interpretation: Higher rates indicate better performance in recognizing and mitigating hallucinations.
methodology.baseline_results: ChatGPT achieved a hallucination recognition accuracy of over 89%.
methodology.validation: Validated through experiments with various state-of-the-art code LLMs.

harm: Functional errors in generated code due to hallucinations.
